raw_consumer_statement: I am filing a complaint regarding another billing dispute involving Citibank and XXXX for merchandise I did not receive, totaling {$820.00}. This dispute involves an order from XXXX XXXX The merchant claims the package was delivered ; however, the only evidence provided is a photograph showing the package left unattended in the lobby of my apartment building, which is a shared, unsecured public space. There was no signature confirmation, no hand delivery, and no proof that I personally received or took possession of the merchandise. I never received this order. Citibank initially issued a provisional credit while investigating. After XXXX submitted the lobby delivery photo, Citibank reversed the credit and held me responsible for the charge, treating delivery to a building lobby as equivalent to delivery to the cardholder. I dispute this conclusion. Delivery to a shared lobby without signature does not establish receipt by the consumer. The merchant selected an unsecured delivery method for high-value merchandise and assumed the risk of loss prior to receipt. I did not authorize delivery to a public lobby and did not receive the merchandise. Citibank advised me to contact the merchant directly. XXXX has refused to refund or replace the order, leaving me responsible for merchandise I never received. This appears to be a repeated failure to properly assess non-receipt disputes where no proof of receipt exists.
